title: adenia lanceolata subsp. scheffleri
intro: Adenia lanceolata subsp. scheffleri, also known as the Yellow Trumpet Vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Passifloraceae. It is traditionally used in medicine due to its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have various medicinal benefits. The plant has been used to treat fever, malaria, and other illnesses, and its extracts have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, potentially offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
